title: backtest-data-prep output
description: Canonical structured output for the backtest-data-prep skill. Layer 1 (the run metadata); the actual dataset lives on disk at the paths in files_written[]. Layer 2 (rendered dataset summary) is described in references/rendering.md.

tier.description: Data fidelity tier. A=paid Stocks Starter+ with flat-files entitlement. B=same plan but flat-files entitlement missing (REST grouped-daily fallback used). Same correctness, different wall-clock.

tier_caveats.description: Human-readable caveats applicable to the chosen tier

run_at.description: UTC timestamp when the dataset was built. Forms part of the manifest reproducibility record.

interface_used.description: Which interface served the daily aggregates. flat-files preferred; rest is the documented fallback when the key isn't entitled (verified 2026-06-23).

label.description: Human-readable description, e.g. 'top 100 by current market cap'

seed.description: CLI flag value used to construct the universe (top100, top500, sp500, custom:<path>)

size.description: Number of tickers in the final universe (post type-filter, post-coverage filter)

type_filter.description: Security type whitelist applied, e.g. 'CS' for common stock only

survivorship_mode.description: clean = delisted-during-window names retained. biased = current-snapshot only. unknown = couldn't determine.

survivorship_note.description: Human-readable explanation of the survivorship treatment

window_start.description: First calendar date requested (window start). The first trading day is the next on-or-after weekday excluding holidays.

window_end.description: Last calendar date requested. Final trading day in the dataset may be earlier when window_end is a weekend/holiday.

trading_days_in_window.description: Count of distinct trading days actually pulled (rows-per-ticker in the parquet for a name with full coverage)

files_written.description: All files emitted to the output directory. Paths are relative to the output dir.

path.description: Path relative to the output directory, e.g. 'ohlcv.parquet'

row_count.description: Row count where meaningful (parquet); null for text files like manifest.md

column_count.description: Column count where meaningful (parquet); null otherwise

bytes_on_disk.description: File size in bytes after Snappy compression

tickers_requested.description: Universe size after seed construction (before any data-quality drops)

tickers_retrieved.description: Universe size after grouped-aggs returned at least one bar; pre-coverage filter

continuous_coverage_count.description: Tickers with a bar on every trading day in the window

partial_coverage_count.description: Tickers with at least one trading day missing (IPO partials, delistings, halts spanning a session)

delisted_during_window_count.description: Tickers in the universe whose delisted_utc falls inside the window. Nonzero is the survivorship signal.

corporate_actions_applied.description: Splits and dividends touched during the window. The skill does NOT re-apply splits to the OHLC columns (Massive's grouped aggs are split-adjusted by default); it emits a cumulative adjustment factor as a separate parquet column so the consumer can un-adjust.

adjustment_method.description: price-only: splits applied by source, cumulative factor emitted for un-adjust. total-return: dividends also adjusted (not implemented in v1). none: raw prints, no factor emitted.

splits.description: Splits with execution_date inside the window

dividends_count.description: Count of cash dividend ex-dates inside the window across the universe

spinoffs.description: Spinoffs detected via the dividend feed (Massive emits spinoffs as stock dividends with type='SO').

spinoff_ticker.description: May be null when the dividend feed records the spinoff but doesn't name the new ticker (Massive sometimes leaves this blank for small spinoffs)

manual_override_recommended.description: Always true in v1; the basis split is not auto-applied

edge_cases.description: Per-name anomalies detected during the run. Operator should review before treating the dataset as 100% backtest-clean.

date.description: Date associated with the event when applicable

detail.description: Human-readable explanation of the edge case

missing_days.description: For ipo_partial_coverage and data_gap, count of trading days missing from the parquet

schema_columns.description: Parquet column names in order. The consumer of the dataset reads this to know what's available without opening the parquet.

take.description: One-paragraph operator-readable verdict: is this dataset backtest-ready, and any caveats a quant should flag before pointing a model at it.

sources.description: All endpoints called during the run, with fetched-at timestamps for audit
